title: TriviaQuest
description: A multiplayer trivia quiz game built with WebSockets for a Networking for Programmers course. Multiple players connect simultaneously, compete through configurable quiz rounds, and see live leaderboard updates in real time.
challenge: Building a real-time multiplayer game that synchronizes question delivery, answer submission, scoring, and leaderboard updates across all connected players simultaneously — without any perceptible lag. The networking layer needed to handle player joins, disconnections, round transitions, and game-over states cleanly.
solution: We used Socket.io for bidirectional WebSocket communication between the Express server and all connected browser clients. The server manages game state centrally — fetching trivia questions from an external API, broadcasting questions to all players, collecting answers within a countdown timer, calculating scores, and pushing live leaderboard updates. We implemented XSS sanitization on all user input and built a settings system allowing the host to configure category, difficulty, and round count.

features.title: Real-Time WebSockets
features.desc: Socket.io bidirectional communication for instant question delivery, answer collection, and live score broadcasting.

features.title: Live Leaderboard
features.desc: Dynamic player ranking with gold/silver/bronze medal system, sorted scores, and visual answer-status indicators.

features.title: Game Configuration
features.desc: Players choose trivia category, difficulty level (Easy/Medium/Hard), and number of rounds before starting.

features.title: Timed Rounds
features.desc: Countdown timer per question with automatic round progression when time expires or all players answer.

features.title: Input Sanitization
features.desc: XSS protection on all user-submitted data using the xss library to prevent injection attacks.

features.title: Auto-Restart
features.desc: Game-over screen with final scores, winner announcement, and automatic redirect to lobby for replay.
